rationale: The pair is closely aligned on core constructs: both papers study no-choice/response-mode effects and process shifts between attribute-based vs alternative-based (comparative vs evaluative) decision processing. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior no-choice/deferral research by testing effects among nondeferring choosers, which matches the cited paper’s contribution on buy/no-buy response mode and deferral mechanisms. With this direct conceptual/mechanism continuity and a DOI-level reference match, this is not an obvious mention-only citation.
